eval-criteria-1.content:
Evaluate `functional.md` for implementation leakage:

- **Why**: Flag if it describes technical gaps (missing APIs, no hooks) instead of human burden (wasted effort, variability, rework)
- **What Changes**: Flag if it describes internal mechanisms rather than artifacts/behaviors users interact with
- **Capabilities**: Flag if framed by component/feature names rather than by actor ("Users can...", "Skill authors can...")
- **User Impact**: Flag if it describes technical footprint (files, APIs, registrations) rather than experience changes

Verbosity often signals implementation leakage - functional specs should be under 1 page.

When citing issues, quote the specific violating text so it can be fixed directly.

severity-cal-1.content:
- **HIGH**: Document-level framing errors that misframe the entire spec (e.g., overview written as implementation spec)
- **MEDIUM**: Item-level issues — individual capabilities, limitations, or sections with wrong framing
- **LOW**: Partial drift in supporting sections that mostly work but have minor leakage
severity-cal-1.justification: Original calibration used generic blocking/degrading criteria; new version calibrates by structural scope which matched the observed severity gradient exactly

critique-technique-00003.content: Include concrete reframings alongside each violation flag
critique-technique-00003.justification: Critics providing reframings achieved 100% usefulness vs flag-only findings

evaluation-criteria-00004.content: Litmus test: would a non-technical stakeholder understand every sentence?
evaluation-criteria-00004.justification: Reusable heuristic that all 4 findings consistently applied to detect implementation leakage
evaluation-criteria-00004.evidence: All 4 findings used this principle to distinguish functional content from implementation details

critique-technique-00005.content: Stay on a single evaluation axis across all findings
critique-technique-00005.justification: Axis discipline is the strongest predictor of critic effectiveness observed
critique-technique-00005.evidence: All 4 findings evaluated implementation leakage only — no drift into completeness or clarity — yielding 100% usefulness

critique-technique-00006.content: Count all findings before writing summary totals
critique-technique-00006.justification: Meta-evaluation showed 3/3 but critic produced 4 findings — undercounting distorts effectiveness scores
critique-technique-00006.evidence: Summary table showed 3/3 useful findings but actual output contained 4 — finding #4 was missed in the count
